accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and the contextual descriptions of Section 1013 (50 U.S.C. 1546a) and Section 601(b) are verifiably accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the description of the cited statutes as providing expedited procedures is correct context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill directs removal of U.S. Armed Forces from unauthorized hostilities within or against Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: The bill does not amend, repeal, or add to existing law; it invokes existing statutory authority, which the summary correctly notes.

exceptions_conditions.why: The summary accurately captures the rule-of-construction carve-outs for self-defense, intelligence sharing, and assistance to attacked partners.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no fixed deadline, and the summary correctly notes there is no fixed deadline while referencing the Feb 28, 2026 date in the carve-outs.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the core removal directive and the rule-of-construction exceptions faithfully.

objective_tone.why: The summary is neutral and descriptive with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, internally consistent, and clearly written.

concise.why: Appropriately concise without significant padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Section 1013 of the Department of State Authorization Act FY1984-1985 and Section 601(b) of the International Security Assistance and Arms Export Control Act of 1976.

correct_figures.why: The February 28, 2026 date and statutory year references are stated correctly.
